by_judge.judge: R NATARAJ IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 16.07.2026

narrative:
On July 16, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 40.0%, with 1,002 cases heard out of 2,505 scheduled across 49 judges. This represents a marginal decrease from the previous day's hearing rate of 40.8% (1,076 heard of 2,635 scheduled) and continues a slight downward trend from the 44.1% rate observed on July 14. 

Among individual judges, Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the heaviest docket, hearing 112 out of 260 scheduled cases (43.1%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 also demonstrated strong clearance, hearing 70 out of 128 cases (54.7%) in his main block, alongside 26 out of 36 cases (72.2%) in an alternate listing. In contrast, Justice B. M. Shyam Prasad in Hall 6 converted only 1 of 125 cases (0.8%) on his largest batch, although he cleared 19 out of 24 cases (79.2%) in a smaller scheduled list.

In terms of hearing stages, "Orders" remained the most active phase, with 410 of 907 scheduled cases heard (45.2%), reflecting an improvement in throughput from yesterday's 40.0% conversion. Conversely, "Final Hearing" stages saw reduced performance, converting only 17.7% (95 of 537) compared to 21.7% on July 15. The "Admission" stage resolved 46.8% of its 404 scheduled cases, down from 52.0% the prior day.

By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) comprised the largest share of the docket, with 319 of 926 cases heard (34.4%), showing a minor clearance improvement over the 33.0% rate on July 15. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) saw 174 of 401 scheduled cases heard (43.4%), while Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) achieved a 47.7% hearing rate (143 of 300), indicating stable daily progress for criminal matters.
